name: St. Josemaría Escrivá
title: Founder of Opus Dei
charism: Sanctification of ordinary work and daily life — finding God in the middle of the world, not apart from it.
biography: St. Josemaría Escrivá founded Opus Dei in 1928, teaching that holiness is not reserved for monks and nuns but is the calling of every baptized Christian in the middle of the world. His book The Way contains 999 maxims for the spiritual life. He emphasized that every honest human activity — from sweeping floors to running a business — can be a path to sanctity when done for the love of God. He promoted a structured 'plan of life' (normas) that laypeople could follow while living in the world.

dailyPractices.title: The Heroic Minute
dailyPractices.description: When the alarm goes off, get up immediately without hesitation. Escrivá called this 'the heroic minute' — conquering yourself at the very start of the day. Offer your first thought to God. The day is won or lost in this moment.

dailyPractices.title: Morning Offering
dailyPractices.description: Immediately upon rising, offer all the works, joys, and sufferings of the day to God. Escrivá taught that this transforms every subsequent action into prayer.

dailyPractices.title: Mental Prayer (15-30 minutes)
dailyPractices.description: Spend time in mental prayer — a personal conversation with God. Escrivá recommended two periods of 15 minutes, morning and evening. Use a spiritual book as a starting point, then speak to God in your own words.

dailyPractices.title: Attend Holy Mass & Communion
dailyPractices.description: The center of the day. Escrivá said: 'The Mass is the center and root of the Christian's spiritual life.' Attend daily if possible. Prepare well before and give thanks after.

dailyPractices.title: Pray the Rosary
dailyPractices.description: Pray the five decades of the Rosary daily. Escrivá often prayed the Rosary while walking. He said it can be prayed anywhere — on the way to work, during a walk, even while waiting.

dailyPractices.title: Sanctify Your Work
dailyPractices.description: Do your professional or domestic work with the greatest human perfection possible AND offer it to God. Escrivá taught three dimensions: work well (competence), work for God (intention), and work to serve others (charity). 'An hour of study, for a modern apostle, is an hour of prayer.'

dailyPractices.title: Aspirations Throughout the Day
dailyPractices.description: Send short prayers ('aspirations') to God throughout the day: 'Serviam!' (I will serve), 'Omnia in bonum' (All things for good), 'Cor Mariae dulcissimum, esto mea salus' (Sweet Heart of Mary, be my salvation). These brief darts of prayer maintain God's presence in the midst of activity.

dailyPractices.title: Spiritual Reading (15 minutes)
dailyPractices.description: Read from the New Testament, a spiritual classic, or The Way. Escrivá said: 'Don't neglect your spiritual reading. Reading has made many saints.'

dailyPractices.title: Evening Examination of Conscience
dailyPractices.description: Review the day: What did I do well? Where did I fail? What can I do better tomorrow? Make an act of contrition. This brief daily examination is the rudder that keeps the spiritual life on course.

dailyPractices.title: Three Hail Marys Before Bed
dailyPractices.description: Pray three Hail Marys for purity before sleep — a simple devotion Escrivá practiced and recommended to everyone.

penances.title: Mortification in Little Things
penances.description: Escrivá emphasized small daily mortifications: getting up on time, finishing a task you dislike, smiling when annoyed, eating less of a favorite food. These hidden conquests build character. 'Don't say: That person bothers me. Think: That person sanctifies me.'

penances.title: Live in Order
penances.description: Keep your workspace clean, your schedule organized, your commitments honored. Escrivá taught that exterior order reflects and supports interior order. Disorder is a subtle form of selfishness.

penances.title: Weekly Confession
penances.description: Confess weekly. Escrivá considered this one of the most important norms. Frequent confession prevents spiritual mediocrity and keeps the soul sensitive to God's grace.

quotes.quote: An hour of study, for a modern apostle, is an hour of prayer.
Don't say: 'That person bothers me.' Think: 'That person sanctifies me.'
Yours is the heroic minute. When the alarm goes off, get up immediately, without hesitation.
Put your heart aside. Duty comes first. But when you fulfill your duty, put your heart into it.
Great souls are able to derive profit from everything.
To begin is for everyone, to persevere is for saints.
There is something holy, something divine hidden in the most ordinary situations, and it is up to each one of you to discover it.
